modules.title: What is Physics?
modules.html:
Physics is the branch of science that studies matter, energy and their interactions.

Main branches

  • Mechanics — motion and forces.
  • Thermodynamics / Heat — energy transfer.
  • Optics — behaviour of light.
  • Electricity & Magnetism — charges, currents, fields.
  • Modern physics — atoms, nuclei, semiconductors.

Careers

Engineering (mechanical, electrical, civil, petroleum), medicine (radiology, medical physics), aviation, telecommunications, teaching, research, meteorology, astronomy.

examples.q: Give one career built on optics.

modules.title: Fundamental & Derived Quantities
modules.html:
A fundamental (base) quantity is defined independently. There are seven SI base quantities:

QuantitySymbolSI Unit
LengthLmetre (m)
MassMkilogram (kg)
TimeTsecond (s)
Electric currentIampere (A)
TemperatureΘkelvin (K)
Amount of substanceNmole (mol)
Luminous intensityJcandela (cd)

Derived quantities are combinations of base quantities, e.g. area (m²), velocity (m/s), force (N = kg·m/s²), energy (J), power (W).

examples.q: Give the SI unit of force.

modules.title: Dimensional Analysis
modules.html:
The dimension of a quantity shows its dependence on base quantities. Length = [L], Mass = [M], Time = [T].

  • Velocity: [L T⁻¹]
  • Acceleration: [L T⁻²]
  • Force: [M L T⁻²]
  • Energy / Work: [M L² T⁻²]
  • Power: [M L² T⁻³]
  • Pressure: [M L⁻¹ T⁻²]

Uses of dimensional analysis: (i) test whether an equation is dimensionally correct, (ii) convert units, (iii) derive relations up to a constant.

Example: check v² = u² + 2as. LHS: [L² T⁻²]. RHS: [L² T⁻²] + [L T⁻²][L] = [L² T⁻²]. ✔

modules.title: Measuring instruments
modules.html:
• Metre rule — length to 1 mm.
  • Vernier caliper — length to 0.01 cm (0.1 mm).
  • Micrometer screw gauge — length to 0.01 mm.
  • Beam / lever balance / electronic balance — mass.
  • Stopwatch — time.
  • Measuring cylinder / eureka can — volume of liquids and irregular solids.

Errors

Systematic errors: repeatable — zero error of instrument, wrongly calibrated scale. Remove by correcting the zero error.

Random errors: unpredictable — parallax, reaction time. Reduce by repeating and averaging.

Accuracy = closeness to true value. Precision = closeness of repeated readings.

examples.q: How do you find the volume of a stone?
examples.a: Displacement method with a measuring cylinder or eureka can.

quiz.q: Which of the following is a fundamental quantity?
quiz.solution: Time is a base SI quantity.

quiz.q: The SI unit of mass is the
quiz.solution: Mass in SI is the kilogram.

quiz.q: The SI unit of force is the
Which is a derived quantity?
quiz.solution: Pressure = force/area, so it is derived.

quiz.q: A physical quantity that has magnitude only is called
Which pair is dimensionally the same?
quiz.solution: Both work and torque have dimension ML²T⁻².

quiz.q: Which equation is dimensionally correct?
quiz.solution: Only v = u + at has consistent [LT⁻¹] on both sides.
